t.hilali-khan: O my Lord! They have indeed led astray many among mankind. But whoso follows me, he verily is of me. And whoso disobeys me, - still You are indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful
t.itani: “My Lord, they have led many people astray. Whoever follows me belongs with me; and whoever disobeys me—You are Forgiving and Merciful.
t.mubarakpuri: "O my Lord! They have indeed led astray many among mankind. But whoso follows me, he verily, is of me. And whoso disobeys me, still You are indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
t.qarib: because, my Lord, they have led my people astray. Hence, anyone who follows me is with me, but, for anyone who disobeys me, You are surely forgiving and Merciful-to-all
t.wahiduddin: My Lord, they have led so many men astray! Anyone who follows me is with me, but if anyone turns against me, You are surely forgiving and merciful
t.shakir: My Lord! surely they have led many men astray; then whoever follows me, he is surely of me, and whoever disobeys me, Thou surely art Forgiving, Merciful
t.haleem: Lord, the [idols] have led many people astray! Anyone who follows me is with me, but as for anyone who disobeys me- You are surely forgiving and merciful
t.maariful: My Lord, they have misled many a people. So, the one who follows me does surely belong to me. As for the one who disobeys me, then You are Most-Forgiving, Very-Merciful
t.khattab: My Lord! They have caused many people to go astray. So whoever follows me is with me, and whoever disobeys me—then surely You are ˹still˺ All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
t.ghali: Lord! Surely they have led many of mankind into error. So whoever follows me, then surely he is of me; and whoever disobeys me, then surely You are Ever-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful
t.irving: My Lord, they have led so many men astray! Anyone who follows me belongs to me, just as anyone who disobeys me [will still find] You are Forgiving, Merciful
t.iwq: My Lord, indeed they have led astray many among the people. So whoever follows me - then he is of me; and whoever disobeys me - indeed, You are yet Forgiving and Merciful

t_id.kemenag: Ya Tuhanku, sesungguhnya berhala-berhala itu telah menyesatkan kebanyakan daripada manusia, maka barangsiapa yang mengikutiku, maka sesungguhnya orang itu termasuk golonganku, dan barangsiapa yang mendurhakai aku, maka sesungguhnya Engkau, Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.
t_id.sabiq: Penyembahan berhala-berhala itu telah menyebabkan kesesatan bagi banyak orang. Maka siapa saja di antara keturunanku yang mengikuti jejakku dan ikhlas beribadah kepada-Mu, adalah benar-benar pengikut agamaku. Tetapi, siapa saja yang tidak menaatiku dan berbuat syirik, maka Engkau Mahakuasa untuk memberinya petunjuk, karena ampunan dan kasih sayang-Mu amat banyak."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Ya Rabbku! Sesungguhnya mereka itu) yakni berhala-berhala itu (telah menyesatkan kebanyakan daripada manusia) karena mereka menyembahnya (maka barang siapa yang mengikutiku) berpegang pada ajaran tauhid (maka sesungguhnya orang itu termasuk golonganku) termasuk pemeluk agamaku (dan barang siapa yang mendurhakai aku, maka sesungguhnya Engkau Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.) pernyataan ini sebelum Nabi Ibrahim mengetahui, bahwa Allah swt. tidak mengampuni dosa syirik.
t_id.iwq: Ya Tuhanku, sesungguhnya berhala-berhala itu telah menyesatkan kebanyakan daripada manusia, maka barangsiapa yang mengikutiku, maka sesungguhnya orang itu termasuk golonganku, dan barangsiapa yang mendurhakai aku, maka sesungguhnya Engkau, Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «رب إنهن» أي الأصنام «أضللن كثيرا من الناس» بعبادتهم لها «فمن تبعني» على التوحيد «فإنه مني» من أهل ديني «ومن عصاني فإنك غفور رحيم» هذا قبل علمه أنه تعالى لا يغفر الشرك
tafsir.siraj-ar: رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ أَضۡلَلۡنَ كَثِيرٗا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِۖ فَمَن تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّيۖ وَمَنۡ عَصَانِي فَإِنَّكَ غَفُورٞ رَّحِيمٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabbku! Sesungguhnya mereka itu) yakni berhala-berhala itu (telah menyesatkan kebanyakan daripada manusia) karena mereka menyembahnya (maka barang siapa yang mengikutiku) berpegang pada ajaran tauhid (maka sesungguhnya orang itu termasuk golonganku) termasuk pemeluk agamaku (dan barang siapa yang mendurhakai aku, maka sesungguhnya Engkau Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.) pernyataan ini sebelum Nabi Ibrahim mengetahui, bahwa Allah swt. tidak mengampuni dosa syirik

ar: رَّبَّنَاۤ إِنِّیۤ أَسۡكَنتُ مِن ذُرِّیَّتِی بِوَادٍ غَیۡرِ ذِی زَرۡعٍ عِندَ بَیۡتِكَ ٱلۡمُحَرَّمِ رَبَّنَا لِیُقِیمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ فَٱجۡعَلۡ أَفۡءِدَةࣰ مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ تَهۡوِیۤ إِلَیۡهِمۡ وَٱرۡزُقۡهُم مِّنَ ٱلثَّمَرَ ٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ یَشۡكُرُونَ
ar_simple: رَبَّنَا إِنِّي أَسْكَنْتُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي بِوَادٍ غَيْرِ ذِي زَرْعٍ عِنْدَ بَيْتِكَ الْمُحَرَّمِ رَبَّنَا لِيُقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ وَارْزُقْهُمْ مِنَ الثَّمَرَاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْكُرُونَ

t.sahih-int: Our Lord, I have settled some of my descendants in an uncultivated valley near Your sacred House, our Lord, that they may establish prayer. So make hearts among the people incline toward them and provide for them from the fruits that they might be grateful
t.asad: O our Sustainer! Behold, I have settled some of my offspring in a valley in which there is no arable land, close to Thy sanctified Temple, so that, O our Sustainer, they might devote themselves to prayer: cause Thou, therefore, people's hearts to incline towards them and grant them fruitful sustenance, so that they might have cause to be grateful
t.yusuf-ali: O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring to dwell in a valley without cultivation, by Thy Sacred House; in order, O our Lord, that they may establish regular Prayer: so fill the hearts of some among men with love towards them, and feed them with fruits: so that they may give thanks
t.pickthall: Our Lord! Lo! I have settled some of my posterity in an uncultivable valley near unto Thy holy House, our Lord! that they may establish proper worship; so incline some hearts of men that they may yearn toward them, and provide Thou them with fruits in order that they may be thankful
t.arberry: Our Lord, I have made some of my seed to dwell in a valley where is no sown land by Thy Holy House; Our Lord, let them perform the prayer, and make hearts of men yearn towards them, and provide them with fruits; haply they will be thankful
t.hilali-khan: O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring to dwell in an uncultivable valley by Your Sacred House (the Ka'bah at Makkah); in order, O our Lord, that they may perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), so fill some hearts among men with love towards them, and (O Allah) provide them with fruits so that they may give thanks
t.itani: “Our Lord, I have settled some of my offspring in a valley of no vegetation, by Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may perform the prayers. So make the hearts of some people incline towards them, and provide them with fruits, that they may be thankful.”
t.mubarakpuri: "O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring dwell in an uncultivable valley by Your Sacred House in order, O our Lord, that they may perform Salah. So fill some hearts among men with love towards them, and (O Allah) provide them with fruits so that they may give thanks.
t.qarib: Lord I have settled some of my children in an uncultivated valley, close to Your Sacred Sanctuary so that, oh our Lord, they might devote themselves to prayer. Make some people's hearts drawn towards them, and provide them with sustenance, so that they may be grateful
t.wahiduddin: O Lord! I have settled some of my offspring in an uncultivable valley near Your Sacred House, Lord, so that they might establish their prayers. So, make people's hearts incline towards them and provide them with fruits so that they may be grateful
t.shakir: O our Lord! surely I have settled a part of my offspring in a valley unproductive of fruit near Thy Sacred House, our Lord! that they may keep up prayer; therefore make the hearts of some people yearn towards them and provide them with fruits; haply they may be grateful
t.haleem: Our Lord, I have established some of my offspring in an uncultivated valley, close to Your Sacred House, Lord, so that they may keep up the prayer. Make people’s hearts turn to them, and provide them with produce, so that they may be thankful
t.maariful: Our Lord, I have settled some of my children in a valley of no vegetation, close to Your sanctified House, so that, Our Lord, they may establish Salāh. So, make hearts of people yearn towards them, and provide them with fruits, so that they may be grateful
t.khattab: Our Lord! I have settled some of my offspring in a barren valley, near Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may establish prayer. So make the hearts of ˹believing˺ people incline towards them and provide them with fruits, so perhaps they will be thankful
t.ghali: Our Lord, surely I have made (some) of my offspring to dwell in a valley that is not under cultivation (Literally: other than that "owning" plantation) at Your Inviolable Home, our Lord, that they may keep up the prayer. So make heart-sights of mankind yearn towards them and provide them with products, that possibly they would thank (You)
t.irving: Our Lord, I have had some of my offspring reside in a valley without any crops alongside Your Hallowed House, our Lord, so that they may keep up prayer. Make men´s vitals feel fond of them, and provide them with fruit so they may act grateful
t.iwq: Our Lord, I have settled some of my descendants in an uncultivated valley near Your sacred House, our Lord, that they may establish prayer. Thus make hearts among the people incline toward them and provide for them from the fruits that they might be grateful

t_id.kemenag: Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya aku telah menempatkan sebahagian keturunanku di lembah yang tidak mempunyai tanam-tanaman di dekat rumah Engkau (Baitullah) yang dihormati, ya Tuhan kami (yang demikian itu) agar mereka mendirikan shalat, maka jadikanlah hati sebagian manusia cenderung kepada mereka dan beri rezekilah mereka dari buah-buahan, mudah-mudahan mereka bersyukur.
t_id.sabiq: "Ya Tuhan kami," kata Ibrâhîm melanjutkan doanya, "aku telah menempatkan sebagian keturunanku di lembah Mekah yang tidak ditumbuhi pepohonan, di rumah-Mu yang Engkau lindungi dari tangan-tangan jahat dan Engkau jadikan daerah sekitarnya aman. Ya Tuhan kami, berilah mereka kehormatan mengerjakan salat di rumah ini. Jadikanlah orang-orang yang berhati mulia mencintai keturunanku itu dengan mengunjungi rumah-Mu. Berilah rezeki berupa buah-buahan yang Engkau kirimkan melalui para pendatang, agar mereka mensyukuri nikmat-Mu dengan mengerjakan salat dan berdoa.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya aku telah menempatkan sebagian keturunanku) sebagian daripada mereka, yaitu Nabi Ismail dan Siti Hajar ibunya (di lembah yang tidak mempunyai tanam-tanaman) yaitu Mekah (di dekat rumah Engkau yang suci) sebelum banjir besar terjadi (ya Rabb agar mereka mendirikan salat, maka jadikanlah hati) kalbu-kalbu (sebagian manusia cenderung) condong dan merindukan (kepada mereka) Sahabat Ibnu Abbas mengatakan, seandainya Nabi Ibrahim mengatakan di dalam doanya, yaitu af-idatan naasi yang artinya semua hati manusia, maka orang-orang Persia, Romawi dan semua manusia niscaya akan cenderung ke Baitullah (dan beri rezekilah mereka dari buah-buahan; mudah-mudahan mereka bersyukur.") dan memang doanya diperkenankan, yaitu dengan disuplaikannya buah-buahan dari Thaif ke Mekah.
t_id.iwq: Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya aku telah menempatkan sebahagian keturunanku di lembah yang tidak mempunyai tanam-tanaman di dekat rumah Engkau (Baitullah) yang dihormati, ya Tuhan kami (yang demikian itu) agar mereka mendirikan salat, maka jadikanlah hati sebagian manusia cenderung kepada mereka dan beri rezekilah mereka dari buah-buahan, mudah-mudahan mereka bersyukur.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ربنا إني أسكنت من ذريتي» أي بعضها وهو إسماعيل مع أمه هاجر «بواد غير ذي زرع» هو مكة «عند بيتك المحرم» الذي كان قبل الطوفان «ربنا ليقيموا الصلاة فاجعل أفئدة» قلوبا «من الناس تهوي» تميل وتحنُّ «إليهم» قال ابن عباس لو قال أفئدة الناس لحنت إليه فارس والروم والناس كلهم «وارزقهم من الثمرات لعلهم يشكرون» وقد فعل بنقل الطائف إليه
tafsir.siraj-ar: رَّبَّنَآ إِنِّيٓ أَسۡكَنتُ مِن ذُرِّيَّتِي بِوَادٍ غَيۡرِ ذِي زَرۡعٍ عِندَ بَيۡتِكَ ٱلۡمُحَرَّمِ رَبَّنَا لِيُقِيمُواْ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ فَٱجۡعَلۡ أَفۡـِٔدَةٗ مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ تَهۡوِيٓ إِلَيۡهِمۡ وَٱرۡزُقۡهُم مِّنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَشۡكُرُونَ (تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ: تَمِيلُ إِلَيْهِمْ، وَتَحِنُّ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya aku telah menempatkan sebagian keturunanku) sebagian daripada mereka, yaitu Nabi Ismail dan Siti Hajar ibunya (di lembah yang tidak mempunyai tanam-tanaman) yaitu Mekah (di dekat rumah Engkau yang suci) sebelum banjir besar terjadi (ya Rabb agar mereka mendirikan salat, maka jadikanlah hati) kalbu-kalbu (sebagian manusia cenderung) condong dan merindukan (kepada mereka) Sahabat Ibnu Abbas mengatakan, seandainya Nabi Ibrahim mengatakan di dalam doanya, yaitu af-idatan naasi yang artinya semua hati manusia, maka orang-orang Persia, Romawi dan semua manusia niscaya akan cenderung ke Baitullah (dan beri rezekilah mereka dari buah-buahan; mudah-mudahan mereka bersyukur.") dan memang doanya diperkenankan, yaitu dengan disuplaikannya buah-buahan dari Thaif ke Mekah

ar: رَبَّنَاۤ إِنَّكَ تَعۡلَمُ مَا نُخۡفِی وَمَا نُعۡلِنُۗ وَمَا یَخۡفَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مِن شَیۡءࣲ فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَا فِی ٱلسَّمَاۤءِ
ar_simple: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ مَا نُخْفِي وَمَا نُعْلِنُ ۗ وَمَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ

t.sahih-int: Our Lord, indeed You know what we conceal and what we declare, and nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the heaven
t.asad: O our Sustainer! Thou truly knowest all that we may hide [in our hearts] as well as all that we bring into the open:' for nothing whatever, be it on earth or in heaven, remains hidden from God
t.yusuf-ali: O our Lord! truly Thou dost know what we conceal and what we reveal: for nothing whatever is hidden from Allah, whether on earth or in heaven
t.pickthall: Our Lord! Lo! Thou knowest that which we hide and that which we proclaim. Nothing in the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah
t.arberry: Our Lord, Thou knowest what we keep secret and what we publish; from God nothing whatever is hidden in earth and heaven
t.hilali-khan: O our Lord! Certainly, You know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah
t.itani: “Our Lord, You know what we conceal and what we reveal. And nothing is hidden from God, on earth or in the heaven.”
t.mubarakpuri: "O our Lord! Certainly, You know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah."
t.qarib: Our Lord, You truly know all that we may hide [in our hearts], as well as all that we bring into the open: for nothing, on earth or in heaven, remains hidden from God
t.wahiduddin: Lord, You have knowledge of all that we hide and all that we reveal: nothing in heaven or on earth is hidden from God
t.shakir: O our Lord! Surely Thou knowest what we hide and what we make public, and nothing in the earth nor any thing in heaven is hidden from Allah
t.haleem: Our Lord, You know well what we conceal and what we reveal: nothing at all is hidden from God, on earth or in heaven
t.maariful: Our Lord, surely You know what we conceal and what we reveal.-Hidden from Allah is nothing whatsoever, neither in the earth nor in the heavens
t.khattab: Our Lord! You certainly know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on earth or in heaven is hidden from Allah
t.ghali: Our Lord, surely You know whatever we conceal and whatever we make public; and in no way is anything whatever concealed from Allah in the earth or in the heaven
t.irving: Our Lord, You know whatever we hide and whatever We display, nothing on earth nor in the sky is hidden from God
t.iwq: Our Lord, verily You know what we conceal and what we declare, and nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the heaven

t_id.kemenag: Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya Engkau mengetahui apa yang kami sembunyikan dan apa yang kami lahirkan; dan tidak ada sesuatupun yang tersembunyi bagi Allah, baik yang ada di bumi maupun yang ada di langit.
t_id.sabiq: Ya Tuhan kami, rahasia dan keterusterangan kami adalah sama dalam ilmu-Mu. Engkau Maha Mengetahui kepentingan-kepentingan kami, dan lebih menyayangi diri kami daripada kami sendiri. Tidak ada sesuatu pun yang ada di langit dan di bumi--sekecil apa pun--yang tersembunyi dari pengetahuan-Mu. Oleh karena itu, sebetulnya kami tidak perlu berdoa. Doa yang kami panjatkan kepada-Mu ini adalah untuk menunjukkan sikap penghambaan, sikap tunduk, dan sikap selalu membutuhkan kami kepada diri-Mu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya Engkau mengetahui apa yang kami sembunyikan) apa yang kami tidak lahirkan (dan apa yang kami lahirkan; dan tidak ada yang tersembunyi bagi Allah) huruf min di sini adalah zaidah (sesuatu pun, baik yang ada di bumi maupun yang ada di langit) ayat ini dapat diartikan kalam Rabb dan dapat pula dianggap sebagai doa Nabi Ibrahim.
t_id.iwq: Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya Engkau mengetahui apa yang kami sembunyikan dan apa yang kami lahirkan; dan tidak ada sesuatupun yang tersembunyi bagi Allah, baik yang ada di bumi maupun yang ada di langit.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ربنا إنك تعلم ما نخفي» نسر «وما نعلن وما يخفى على الله من» زائدة «شيء في الأرض ولا في السماء» يحتمل أن يكون من كلامه تعالى أو كلام إبراهيم
tafsir.siraj-ar: رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ تَعۡلَمُ مَا نُخۡفِي وَمَا نُعۡلِنُۗ وَمَا يَخۡفَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مِن شَيۡءٖ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَا فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya Engkau mengetahui apa yang kami sembunyikan) apa yang kami tidak lahirkan (dan apa yang kami lahirkan; dan tidak ada yang tersembunyi bagi Allah) huruf min di sini adalah zaidah (sesuatu pun, baik yang ada di bumi maupun yang ada di langit) ayat ini dapat diartikan kalam Rabb dan dapat pula dianggap sebagai doa Nabi Ibrahim

ar: ٱلۡحَمۡدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِی وَهَبَ لِی عَلَى ٱلۡكِبَرِ إِسۡمَـٰعِیلَ وَإِسۡحَـٰقَۚ إِنَّ رَبِّی لَسَمِیعُ ٱلدُّعَاۤءِ
ar_simple: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي وَهَبَ لِي عَلَى الْكِبَرِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّي لَسَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ

t.sahih-int: Praise to Allah, who has granted to me in old age Ishmael and Isaac. Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of supplication
t.asad: All praise is due to God, who has bestowed upon me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac! Behold, my Sustainer hears indeed all prayer
t.yusuf-ali: Praise be to Allah, Who hath granted unto me in old age Isma'il and Isaac: for truly my Lord is He, the Hearer of Prayer
t.pickthall: Praise be to Allah Who hath given me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac! Lo! my Lord is indeed the Hearer of Prayer
t.arberry: Praise be to God, who has given me, though I am old, Ishmael and Isaac; surely my Lord hears the petition
t.hilali-khan: All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has given me in old age Isma'il (Ishmael) and Ishaque (Isaac). Verily! My Lord is indeed the All-Hearer of invocations
t.itani: “Praise be to God, Who has given me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac. My Lord is the Hearer of Prayers.”
t.mubarakpuri: "All praise is due to Allah, Who has given me in old age Isma'il and Ishaq. Verily, my Lord is indeed the All-Hearer of invocations."
t.qarib: All praise is due to God, who has granted me Ishmael and Isaac in my old age. My Lord hears all prayer
t.wahiduddin: Praise be to God who has bestowed upon me, despite my old age, Ishmael and Isaac. Surely my Lord is the hearer of prayer
t.shakir: Praise be to Allah, Who has given me in old age Ismail and Ishaq; most surely my Lord is the Hearer of prayer
t.haleem: Praise be to God, who has granted me Ishmael and Isaac in my old age: my Lord hears all requests
t.maariful: Praise be to Allah who, despite my old age, blessed me with Ismā‘īl (Ishmael) and IsHāq (Isaac). Surely, my Lord is the One who listens to the prayer
t.khattab: All praise is for Allah who has blessed me with Ishmael and Isaac in my old age. My Lord is indeed the Hearer of ˹all˺ prayers
t.ghali: Praise be to Allah, Who has bestowed upon me, though being great with (years), (i.e., though I am old) Shuaayb and Ishaq; (Ishmael and Isaac, respectively) surely my Lord is indeed Ever-Hearing of invocation
t.irving: Praise be to God Who has bestowed Ishmael and Isaac on me in my old age. My Lord is so Alert to anyone´s appeal
t.iwq: Praise to Allah, who has granted to me in old age Ishmael and Isaac. Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of supplication

t_id.kemenag: Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menganugerahkan kepadaku di hari tua(ku) Ismail dan Ishaq. Sesungguhnya Tuhanku, benar-benar Maha Mendengar (memperkenankan) doa.
t_id.sabiq: Segala puji bagi Allah yang, dengan umurku yang tua renta dan tidak ada harapan mempunyai anak lagi ini, telah mengaruniakanku Ismâ'îl dan Ishâq. Tuhanku benar-benar Maha Mendengarkan dan mengabulkan doaku."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menganugerahkan kepada diriku) memberiku (sekalipun) walaupun (sudah tua, Ismail) Nabi Ismail dilahirkan sewaktu Nabi Ibrahim berumur sembilan puluh sembilan tahun (dan Ishak.) dilahirkan sewaktu Nabi Ibrahim berumur seratus dua belas tahun (Sesungguhnya Rabbku benar-benar Maha Mendengar doa).
t_id.iwq: Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menganugerahkan kepadaku di hari tua(ku) Ismail dan Ishaq. Sesungguhnya Tuhanku, benar-benar Maha Mendengar (memperkenankan) doa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «الحمد لله الذي وهب لي» أعطاني «على» مع «الكبر إسماعيل» ولد وله تسع وتسعون سنة «وإسحاق» ولد وله مائة واثنتا عشرة سنة «إن ربي لسميع الدعاء»
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلۡحَمۡدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي وَهَبَ لِي عَلَى ٱلۡكِبَرِ إِسۡمَٰعِيلَ وَإِسۡحَٰقَۚ إِنَّ رَبِّي لَسَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menganugerahkan kepada diriku) memberiku (sekalipun) walaupun (sudah tua, Ismail) Nabi Ismail dilahirkan sewaktu Nabi Ibrahim berumur sembilan puluh sembilan tahun (dan Ishak.) dilahirkan sewaktu Nabi Ibrahim berumur seratus dua belas tahun (Sesungguhnya Rabbku benar-benar Maha Mendengar doa)

ar: رَبِّ ٱجۡعَلۡنِی مُقِیمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّیَّتِیۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلۡ دُعَاۤءِ
ar_simple: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَاءِ

t.sahih-int: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication
t.asad: [hence,] O my Sustainer, cause me and [some] of my offspring to remain constant in prayer! "And, O our Sustainer, accept this my prayer
t.yusuf-ali: O my Lord! make me one who establishes regular Prayer, and also (raise such) among my offspring O our Lord! and accept Thou my Prayer
t.pickthall: My Lord! Make me to establish proper worship, and some of my posterity (also); our Lord! and accept my prayer
t.arberry: My Lord, make me a performer of the prayer, and of my seed. Our Lord, and receive my petition
t.hilali-khan: O my Lord! Make me one who performs As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and (also) from my offspring, our Lord! And accept my invocation
t.itani: “My Lord, make me one who performs the prayer, and from my offspring. Our Lord, accept my supplication.”
t.mubarakpuri: "O my Lord! Make me one who performs Salah, and (also) from my offspring, our Lord! And accept my invocation."
t.qarib: Lord, grant that I may perform prayer, and so may my children. "Lord accept my supplication
t.wahiduddin: Lord, grant that I may keep up the prayer, and so may my offspring. My Lord, accept my prayer
t.shakir: My Lord! make me keep up prayer and from my offspring (too), O our Lord, and accept my prayer
t.haleem: Lord, grant that I and my offspring may keep up the prayer. Our Lord, accept my request
t.maariful: My Lord, make me steadfast in Salāh, and my offspring as well. And, Our Lord, grant my prayer
t.khattab: My Lord! Make me and those ˹believers˺ of my descendants keep up prayer. Our Lord! Accept my prayers
t.ghali: Lord! Make me one who keeps up the prayer and of my offspring (ones who keep up the prayer), our Lord, and graciously accept my invocation
t.irving: My Lord, make me keep up prayer, and have my offspring [do so too]
t.iwq: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and many from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication

t_id.kemenag: Ya Tuhanku, jadikanlah aku dan anak cucuku orang-orang yang tetap mendirikan shalat, ya Tuhan kami, perkenankanlah doaku.
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm kemudian melanjutkan, "Ya Tuhanku, bimbinglah aku untuk mengerjakan salat dengan cara yang benar, juga anak keturunanku yang baik-baik. Ya Tuhanku, kabulkanlah doaku!
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabbku! Jadikanlah aku orang-orang yang tetap mendirikan salat dan) jadikan pula (anak cucuku) orang-orang yang tetap mendirikannya. Nabi Ibrahim sengaja di dalam doanya ini memakai ungkapan min yang menunjukkan makna sebagian karena Allah swt. telah memberitahukan kepadanya bahwa di antara anak cucunya itu terdapat orang yang kafir (Ya Rabb kami! Kabulkanlah doaku) semua doa yang telah disebutkan tadi.
t_id.iwq: Ya Tuhanku, jadikanlah aku dan anak cucuku orang-orang yang tetap mendirikan salat, ya Tuhan kami, perkenankanlah doaku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «رب اجعلني مقيم الصلاة و» اجعل «من ذريتي» ومن يقيمها وأتى بمن لإعلام الله تعالى له أن منهم كفارا «ربنا وتقبل دعاء» المذكور
tafsir.siraj-ar: رَبِّ ٱجۡعَلۡنِي مُقِيمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِيۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلۡ دُعَآءِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabbku! Jadikanlah aku orang-orang yang tetap mendirikan salat dan) jadikan pula (anak cucuku) orang-orang yang tetap mendirikannya. Nabi Ibrahim sengaja di dalam doanya ini memakai ungkapan min yang menunjukkan makna sebagian karena Allah swt. telah memberitahukan kepadanya bahwa di antara anak cucunya itu terdapat orang yang kafir (Ya Rabb kami! Kabulkanlah doaku) semua doa yang telah disebutkan tadi

ar: رَبَّنَا ٱغۡفِرۡ لِی وَلِوَ ٰلِدَیَّ وَلِلۡمُؤۡمِنِینَ یَوۡمَ یَقُومُ ٱلۡحِسَابُ
ar_simple: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسَابُ

t.sahih-int: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and the believers the Day the account is established
t.asad: Grant Thy forgiveness unto me, and my parents, and all the believers, on the Day on which the [last] reckoning will come to pass
t.yusuf-ali: O our Lord! cover (us) with Thy Forgiveness - me, my parents, and (all) Believers, on the Day that the Reckoning will be established
t.pickthall: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents and believers on the day when the account is cast
t.arberry: Our Lord, forgive Thou me and my parents, and the believers, upon the day when the reckoning shall come to pass
t.hilali-khan: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (all) the believers on the Day when the reckoning will be established
t.itani: “Our Lord, forgive me, and my parents, and the believers, on the Day the Reckoning takes place.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (all) the believers on the Day when the reckoning will be established."
t.qarib: Grant Your forgiveness to me, and my parents, and all the believers, on the Day of Reckoning
t.wahiduddin: Forgive me, Lord, and forgive my parents and all the believers on the Day of Reckoning
t.shakir: O our Lord! grant me protection and my parents and the believers on the day when the reckoning shall come to pass
t.haleem: Our Lord, forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day of Reckoning.’
t.maariful: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and all believers on the day when reckoning shall take place.”
t.khattab: Our Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day when the judgment will come to pass.”
t.ghali: Our Lord, forgive me, and my parents, and the believers upon the Day when the reckoning will come up
t.irving: Our Lord, accept my appeal! Our Lord, forgive me, both my parents, and believers on the Day when the Reckoning will be set up
t.iwq: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and those who believe the Day the account is established

t_id.kemenag: Ya Tuhan kami, beri ampunlah aku dan kedua ibu bapaku dan sekalian orang-orang mukmin pada hari terjadinya hisab (hari kiamat)".
t_id.sabiq: Ya Tuhan kami, ampunilah aku atas dosa-dosa yang telah aku lakukan. Ampuni juga kedua orangtuaku dan orang-orang Mukmin pada hari dilaksanakannya perhitungan, yang diikuti dengan pembalasan."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Beri ampunlah aku dan kedua ibu bapakku) doa ini diucapkan sebelum jelas bagi Nabi Ibrahim bahwa kedua orang tuanya memusuhi Allah swt. Akan tetapi menurut suatu pendapat dikatakan bahwa ibu Nabi Ibrahim masuk Islam. Lafal waalidayya menurut qiraat yang lain dapat dibaca mufrad sehingga bacaannya menjadi waalidiy (dan sekalian orang-orang mukmin pada hari terjadinya) ditegakkannya (hisab) selanjutnya Allah berfirman:
t_id.iwq: Ya Tuhan kami, beri ampunlah aku dan kedua ibu bapaku dan sekalian orang-orang mukmin pada hari terjadinya hisab (hari kiamat)".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ربنا اغفر لي ولوالدي» هذا قبل أن يتبين له عداوتهما لله عز وجل وقيل أسلمت أمه وقرئ والدي مفردا وولدي «وللمؤمنين يوم يقوم» يثبت «الحساب» قال تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: رَبَّنَا ٱغۡفِرۡ لِي وَلِوَٰلِدَيَّ وَلِلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ يَوۡمَ يَقُومُ ٱلۡحِسَابُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Beri ampunlah aku dan kedua ibu bapakku) doa ini diucapkan sebelum jelas bagi Nabi Ibrahim bahwa kedua orang tuanya memusuhi Allah swt. Akan tetapi menurut suatu pendapat dikatakan bahwa ibu Nabi Ibrahim masuk Islam. Lafal waalidayya menurut qiraat yang lain dapat dibaca mufrad sehingga bacaannya menjadi waalidiy (dan sekalian orang-orang mukmin pada hari terjadinya) ditegakkannya (hisab) selanjutnya Allah berfirman

ar: وَلَا تَحۡسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَـٰفِلًا عَمَّا یَعۡمَلُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَۚ إِنَّمَا یُؤَخِّرُهُمۡ لِیَوۡمࣲ تَشۡخَصُ فِیهِ ٱلۡأَبۡصَـٰرُ
ar_simple: وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّهَ غَافِلًا عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ الظَّالِمُونَ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ تَشْخَصُ فِيهِ الْأَبْصَارُ

t.sahih-int: And never think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them for a Day when eyes will stare [in horror]
t.asad: AND DO NOT think that God is unaware of what the evildoers are doing: He but grants them respite until the Day when their eyes will stare in horror
t.yusuf-ali: Think not that Allah doth not heed the deeds of those who do wrong. He but giveth them respite against a Day when the eyes will fixedly stare in horror
t.pickthall: Deem not that Allah is unaware of what the wicked do. He but giveth them a respite till a day when eyes will stare (in terror)
t.arberry: Deem not that God is heedless of what the evildoers work; He is only deferring them to a day when eyes shall stare
t.hilali-khan: Consider not that Allah is unaware of that which the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, etc.) do, but He gives them respite up to a Day when the eyes will stare in horror
t.itani: Do not ever think that God is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only defers them until a Day when the sights stare.
t.mubarakpuri: Consider not that Allah is unaware of that which the wrongdoers do, but He gives them respite up to a Day when the eyes will stare in horror.
t.qarib: Do not think [Prophet] that God is unaware of what the unjust do; He only gives them respite until the Day when eyes will stare in horror
t.wahiduddin: Do not think God is unaware of the wrongdoers' actions. He only gives them respite till the Day on which all eyes will stare fixedly in horror
t.shakir: And do not think Allah to be heedless of what the unjust do; He only respites them to a day on which the eyes shall be fixedly open
t.haleem: Do not think [Prophet] that God is unaware of what the disbelievers do: He only gives them respite until a Day when their eyes will stare in terror
t.maariful: Never think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers are doing. He is but giving them respite up to a day when the eyes shall remain upraised (in terror)
t.khattab: Do not think ˹O Prophet˺ that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them until a Day when ˹their˺ eyes will stare in horror—
t.ghali: And definitely do not reckon that Allah is heedless of what the unjust (ones) are doing; surely He only defers them to a Day (when) be holdings will glaze over
t.irving: Do not reckon God is oblivious of what wrongdoers are doing; He is merely putting them off till a day when their eyesight will be glazed
t.iwq: And never think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them for a Day when eyes will stare in horror

t_id.kemenag: Dan janganlah sekali-kali kamu (Muhammad) mengira, bahwa Allah lalai dari apa yang diperbuat oleh orang-orang yang zalim. Sesungguhnya Allah memberi tangguh kepada mereka sampai hari yang pada waktu itu mata (mereka) terbelalak,
t_id.sabiq: Janganlah engkau sekali-kali mengira, wahai Muhammad, bahwa Tuhan-Mu lupa akan penyerangan yang dilakukan oleh orang-orang zalim terhadap umat Islam. Dia mengetahui kesalahan mereka itu. Hanya saja Dia menunda hukuman-Nya kepada mereka sampai pada suatu hari yang penuh kesusahan. Yaitu hari di mana mereka kehilangan kendali terhadap mata mereka, hingga terus membelalak dan tidak bisa terpejam karena apa yang mereka lihat sangat menakutkan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan janganlah sekali-kali kamu mengira bahwa Allah lalai dari apa yang diperbuat oleh orang-orang yang lalim) yakni orang-orang kafir dari kalangan penduduk Mekah. (Sesungguhnya Allah hanya memberi tangguh kepada mereka) tanpa diazab (sampai hari yang pada waktu itu mata mereka terbelalak) karena ngerinya pemandangan yang mereka saksikan kala itu. Di dalam istilah bahasa atau lugah jika dikatakan bashara fulaanun syakhshan, artinya si polan melihat seseorang tanpa mengedipkan matanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan janganlah sekali-kali kamu (Muhammad) mengira, bahwa Allah lalai dari apa yang diperbuat oleh orang-orang yang zalim. Sesungguhnya Allah memberi tangguh kepada mereka sampai hari yang pada waktu itu mata (mereka) terbelalak,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولا تحسبن الله غافلا عما يعمل الظالمون» الكافرين من أهل مكة «إنما يؤخرهم» بلا عذاب «ليوم تشخص فيه الأبصار» لهول ما ترى يقال شخص بصر فلان أي فتحه فلم يغمضه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَا تَحۡسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَٰفِلًا عَمَّا يَعۡمَلُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَۚ إِنَّمَا يُؤَخِّرُهُمۡ لِيَوۡمٖ تَشۡخَصُ فِيهِ ٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرُ (تَشْخَصُ: تَرْتَفِعُ عُيُونُهُمْ فِيهِ، وَلَا تَغْمَضُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan janganlah sekali-kali kamu mengira bahwa Allah lalai dari apa yang diperbuat oleh orang-orang yang lalim) yakni orang-orang kafir dari kalangan penduduk Mekah. (Sesungguhnya Allah hanya memberi tangguh kepada mereka) tanpa diazab (sampai hari yang pada waktu itu mata mereka terbelalak) karena ngerinya pemandangan yang mereka saksikan kala itu. Di dalam istilah bahasa atau lugah jika dikatakan bashara fulaanun syakhshan, artinya si polan melihat seseorang tanpa mengedipkan matanya

ar: مُهۡطِعِینَ مُقۡنِعِی رُءُوسِهِمۡ لَا یَرۡتَدُّ إِلَیۡهِمۡ طَرۡفُهُمۡۖ وَأَفۡءِدَتُهُمۡ هَوَاۤءࣱ
ar_simple: مُهْطِعِينَ مُقْنِعِي رُءُوسِهِمْ لَا يَرْتَدُّ إِلَيْهِمْ طَرْفُهُمْ ۖ وَأَفْئِدَتُهُمْ هَوَاءٌ

t.sahih-int: Racing ahead, their heads raised up, their glance does not come back to them, and their hearts are void
t.asad: the while they will be running confusedly to and fro, with their heads upraised [in supplication], unable to look away from what they shall behold, and their hearts an abysmal void
t.yusuf-ali: They running forward with necks outstretched, their heads uplifted, their gaze returning not towards them, and their hearts a (gaping) void
t.pickthall: As they come hurrying on in fear, their heads upraised, their gaze returning not to them, and their hearts as air
t.arberry: when they shall run with necks outstretched and heads erect, their glances never returned on themselves, their hearts void
t.hilali-khan: (They will be) hastening forward with necks outstretched, their heads raised up (towards the sky), their gaze returning not towards them and their hearts empty (from thinking because of extreme fear)
t.itani: Their necks outstretched, their heads upraised, their gaze unblinking, their hearts void.
t.mubarakpuri: (They will be) hastening forward with necks outstretched, their heads raised up (towards the sky), their gaze returning not towards them and their hearts empty.
t.qarib: They run around confusedly, with their heads up, unable to look away, their hearts a gaping void
t.wahiduddin: They will hurry on in fright, their heads lifted up, their gaze directed forward, their minds utterly void
t.shakir: Hastening forward, their heads upraised, their eyes not reverting to them and their hearts vacant
t.haleem: They will rush forward, craning their necks, unable to divert their eyes, a gaping void in their hearts
t.maariful: They shall be rushing with their heads raised upward; their eyes shall not return towards them and their hearts shall be hollow
t.khattab: rushing forth, heads raised, never blinking, hearts void
t.ghali: Hurrying with their heads protruding, their glances not returning towards them, and their heart-sights (as) air
t.irving: as they dash along with their heads tilted forward, their glances not checking back for them, and their vital organs gaping open
t.iwq: Racing ahead, their heads raised up, their glance does not come back to them, and their hearts are void

t_id.kemenag: mereka datang bergegas-gegas memenuhi panggilan dengan mangangkat kepalanya, sedang mata mereka tidak berkedip-kedip dan hati mereka kosong.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka lalu cepat-cepat menghampiri penyeru dengan kepala mendongak ke langit, mata membelalak tak terkendali, dan hati kosong tanpa pikiran apa-apa, karena rasa takut yang sangat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka datang bergegas) dengan segera memenuhi panggilan. Lafal ayat ini berkedudukan menjadi hal atau kata keterangan (dengan mengangkat) mendongakkan (kepalanya) ke langit (sedangkan mata mereka tidak berkedip-kedip) melotot (dan hati mereka kosong) tidak ada suatu pikiran pun yang terbetik di dalamnya saking kaget dan ngerinya.
t_id.iwq: mereka datang bergegas-gegas memenuhi panggilan dengan mangangkat kepalanya, sedang mata mereka tidak berkedip-kedip dan hati mereka kosong.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «مهطعين» مسرعين حال «مقنعي» رافعي «رءُوسهم» إلى السماء «لا يرتد إليهم طرفهم» بصرهم «وأفئدتهم» قلوبهم «هواء» خالية من العقل لفزعهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: مُهۡطِعِينَ مُقۡنِعِي رُءُوسِهِمۡ لَا يَرۡتَدُّ إِلَيۡهِمۡ طَرۡفُهُمۡۖ وَأَفۡـِٔدَتُهُمۡ هَوَآءٞ (مُهْطِعِينَ: مُسْرِعِينَ, مُقْنِعِي رُؤُوسِهِمْ: رَافِعِي رُؤُوسِهِمْ, وَأَفْئِدَتُهُمْ هَوَاءٌ: قُلُوبُهُمْ خَالِيَةٌ مِنْ شِدَّةِ الهَوْلِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka datang bergegas) dengan segera memenuhi panggilan. Lafal ayat ini berkedudukan menjadi hal atau kata keterangan (dengan mengangkat) mendongakkan (kepalanya) ke langit (sedangkan mata mereka tidak berkedip-kedip) melotot (dan hati mereka kosong) tidak ada suatu pikiran pun yang terbetik di dalamnya saking kaget dan ngerinya

ar: وَأَنذِرِ ٱلنَّاسَ یَوۡمَ یَأۡتِیهِمُ ٱلۡعَذَابُ فَیَقُولُ ٱلَّذِینَ ظَلَمُوا۟ رَبَّنَاۤ أَخِّرۡنَاۤ إِلَىٰۤ أَجَلࣲ قَرِیبࣲ نُّجِبۡ دَعۡوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ ٱلرُّسُلَۗ أَوَ لَمۡ تَكُونُوۤا۟ أَقۡسَمۡتُم مِّن قَبۡلُ مَا لَكُم مِّن زَوَالࣲ
ar_simple: وَأَنْذِرِ النَّاسَ يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ الْعَذَابُ فَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رَبَّنَا أَخِّرْنَا إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ نُجِبْ دَعْوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ الرُّسُلَ ۗ أَوَلَمْ تَكُونُوا أَقْسَمْتُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ مَا لَكُمْ مِنْ زَوَالٍ

t.sahih-int: And, [O Muhammad], warn the people of a Day when the punishment will come to them and those who did wrong will say, "Our Lord, delay us for a short term; we will answer Your call and follow the messengers." [But it will be said], "Had you not sworn, before, that for you there would be no cessation
t.asad: Hence, warn men of the Day when this suffering may befall them, and when those who did wrong [in their lifetime] will exclaim: "O our Sustainer! Grant us respite for a short while, so that we might respond to Thy call and follow the apostles!" [But God will answer:] "Why - were you not aforetime wont to swear that no kind of resurrection and retribution awaited you
t.yusuf-ali: So warn mankind of the Day when the Wrath will reach them: then will the wrong-doers say: "Our Lord! respite us (if only) for a short term: we will answer Thy call, and follow the messengers!" "What! were ye not wont to swear aforetime that ye should suffer no decline
t.pickthall: And warn mankind of a day when the doom will come upon them, and those who did wrong will say: Our Lord! Reprieve us for a little while. We will obey Thy call and will follow the messengers. (It will be answered): Did ye not swear before that there would be no end for you
t.arberry: And warn mankind of the day when the chastisement comes on them, And those who did evil shall say, 'Our Lord, defer us to a near term, and we will answer Thy call, and follow the Messengers.' 'Ah, but did you not swear aforetime there should be no removing for you
t.hilali-khan: And warn (O Muhammad SAW) mankind of the Day when the torment will come unto them; then the wrong-doers will say: "Our Lord! Respite us for a little while, we will answer Your Call and follow the Messengers!" (It will be said): "Had you not sworn aforetime that you would not leave (the world for the Hereafter)
t.itani: And warn mankind of the Day when the punishment will come upon them, and the wicked will say, “Our Lord, defer us for a little while, and we will answer Your call and follow the messengers.” Did you not swear before that there will be no passing away for you?
t.mubarakpuri: And warn mankind of the Day when the torment will come unto them; then the wrongdoers will say: "Our Lord! Respite us for a little while, we will answer Your call and follow the Messengers!" (It will be said:) "Had you not sworn aforetime that you would not leave (the world for the Hereafter)."
t.qarib: Warn people of the Day when this suffering may befall them, and when the unjust will say, "Our Lord, grant us respite for a little more time, so that we might respond to Your call and follow the Messengers." [God will say,] "Did you not swear in the past that your power would have no end
t.wahiduddin: Warn men of the Day when the punishment will come upon them, and when the wrongdoers will say, "Our Lord, grant us respite for a short while. We will respond to Your call and will follow the messengers." [But God will answer], are you not those who swore that you would never suffer any decline
t.shakir: And warn people of the day when the chastisement shall come to them, then those who were unjust will say: O our Lord! respite us to a near term, (so) we shall respond to Thy call and follow the apostles. What! did you not swear before (that) there will be no passing away for you
t.haleem: So warn people of the Day when punishment will come to them, and when the disbelievers will say, ‘Our Lord, give us a little more time: we shall answer Your call and follow the messengers.’ Did you [disbelievers] not swear in the past that your power would have no end
t.maariful: Warn the people of a day when punishment will come upon them, and the wrongdoers will say, “Our Lord, give us respite for a short term, and we will respond to Your call and will follow the messengers.” (Allah will say), “Is it not that you had sworn oaths earlier that you would not have to leave (the world)
t.khattab: And warn the people of the Day when the punishment will overtake ˹the wicked among˺ them, and the wrongdoers will cry, “Our Lord! Delay us for a little while. We will respond to Your call and follow the messengers!” ˹It will be said,˺ “Did you not swear before that you would never be removed ˹to the next life˺?”
t.ghali: And warn mankind of the Day when the torment will come up to them. Then the ones who did (an) injustice will say, "Our Lord, defer us to a near term, (then) we will answer Your Call and closely follow the Messengers." (But) did you not swear earlier that in no way will you (face) extinction
t.irving: Warn mankind against a day when torment will come upon them. Those who have done wrong will say: "Our Lord, put us off for a short while; we will answer Your appeal and follow the messengers!" (Did you not use to swear previously that you would never [face] extinction
t.iwq: And, O Muhammad, warn the people of a Day when the punishment will come to them and those who did wrong will say, "Our Lord, delay us for a short term; we will answer Your call and follow the messengers." But it will be said, "Had you not sworn, before, that for you there would be no cessation

t_id.kemenag: Dan berikanlah peringatan kepada manusia terhadap hari (yang pada waktu itu) datang azab kepada mereka, maka berkatalah orang-orang yang zalim: "Ya Tuhan kami, beri tangguhlah kami (kembalikanlah kami ke dunia) walaupun dalam waktu yang sedikit, niscaya kami akan mematuhi seruan Engkau dan akan mengikuti rasul-rasul". (Kepada mereka dikatakan): "Bukankah kamu telah bersumpah dahulu (di dunia) bahwa sekali-kali kamu tidak akan binasa?
t_id.sabiq: Hai Muhammad, terangkan kepada manusia hal-hal yang menakutkan pada hari kiamat ketika mereka disiksa, saat orang-orang yang menganiaya diri mereka dengan kekafiran dan perbuatan maksiat berkata, "Ya Tuhan kami, tundalah penyiksaan kami, kembalikanlah kami ke dunia, berilah kami waktu lagi barang sedikit, agar kami memperbaiki kelalaian kami dalam memenuhi seruan-Mu tentang tauhid dan mengikuti utusan-utusan-Mu." Allah berkata kepada mereka, "Apakah hari ini kalian berkata begitu dan lupa bahwa di dunia dulu kalian pernah bersumpah bahwa apabila kalian mati, kemudian datang hari pembangkitan, kenikmatan yang kalian rasakan itu tidak akan hilang?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan berikanlah peringatan) hai Muhammad, peringatkanlah (kepada manusia) yakni orang-orang kafir (terhadap hari yang pada waktu itu datang azab kepada mereka) yaitu hari kiamat (maka berkatalah orang-orang yang lalim) yakni orang-orang kafir ("Ya Rabb kami! Beri tangguhlah kami) seumpamanya Engkau mengembalikan kami ke dunia (walaupun dalam waktu yang sedikit, niscaya kami akan mematuhi seruan Engkau) dengan mengamalkan ajaran tauhid (dan akan mengikuti rasul-rasul.") lalu dikatakan kepada mereka dengan nada celaan ("Bukankah kalian telah bersumpah) telah berikrar (sebelumnya dahulu) yaitu sewaktu di dunia (bahwa sekali-kali kalian tidak akan) huruf min di sini adalah zaidah (binasa?") setelah meninggalkan dunia menuju ke akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Dan berikanlah peringatan kepada manusia terhadap hari (yang pada waktu itu) datang azab kepada mereka, maka berkatalah orang-orang yang zalim: "Ya Tuhan kami, beri tangguhlah kami (kembalikanlah kami ke dunia) walaupun dalam waktu yang sedikit, niscaya kami akan mematuhi seruan Engkau dan akan mengikuti rasul-rasul". (Kepada mereka dikatakan): "Bukankah kamu telah bersumpah dahulu (di dunia) bahwa sekali-kali kamu tidak akan binasa?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأنذر» خوِّف يا محمد «الناس» الكفار «يوم يأتيهم العذاب» هو يوم القيامة «فيقول الذين ظلموا» كفروا «ربنا أخرنا» بأن تردنا إلى الدنيا «إلى أجل قريب نجب دعوتك» بالتوحيد «ونتبع الرسل» فيقال لهم توبيخا «أو لم تكونوا أقسمتم» حلفتم «من قبل» في الدنيا «ما لكم من» زائدة «زوال» عنها إلى الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَنذِرِ ٱلنَّاسَ يَوۡمَ يَأۡتِيهِمُ ٱلۡعَذَابُ فَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ رَبَّنَآ أَخِّرۡنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٖ قَرِيبٖ نُّجِبۡ دَعۡوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ ٱلرُّسُلَۗ أَوَلَمۡ تَكُونُوٓاْ أَقۡسَمۡتُم مِّن قَبۡلُ مَا لَكُم مِّن زَوَالٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan berikanlah peringatan) hai Muhammad, peringatkanlah (kepada manusia) yakni orang-orang kafir (terhadap hari yang pada waktu itu datang azab kepada mereka) yaitu hari kiamat (maka berkatalah orang-orang yang lalim) yakni orang-orang kafir ("Ya Rabb kami! Beri tangguhlah kami) seumpamanya Engkau mengembalikan kami ke dunia (walaupun dalam waktu yang sedikit, niscaya kami akan mematuhi seruan Engkau) dengan mengamalkan ajaran tauhid (dan akan mengikuti rasul-rasul.") lalu dikatakan kepada mereka dengan nada celaan ("Bukankah kalian telah bersumpah) telah berikrar (sebelumnya dahulu) yaitu sewaktu di dunia (bahwa sekali-kali kalian tidak akan) huruf min di sini adalah zaidah (binasa?") setelah meninggalkan dunia menuju ke akhirat

ar: وَسَكَنتُمۡ فِی مَسَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِینَ ظَلَمُوۤا۟ أَنفُسَهُمۡ وَتَبَیَّنَ لَكُمۡ كَیۡفَ فَعَلۡنَا بِهِمۡ وَضَرَبۡنَا لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَمۡثَالَ
ar_simple: وَسَكَنْتُمْ فِي مَسَاكِنِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنَا بِهِمْ وَضَرَبْنَا لَكُمُ الْأَمْثَالَ

t.sahih-int: And you lived among the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it had become clear to you how We dealt with them. And We presented for you [many] examples
t.asad: And yet, you dwelt in the dwelling-places of those who had sinned against their own selves [before your time], and it was made obvious to you how We had dealt with them: for We have set forth unto you many a parable [of sin, resurrection and divine retribution]
t.yusuf-ali: And ye dwelt in the dwellings of men who wronged their own souls; ye were clearly shown how We dealt with them; and We put forth (many) parables in your behoof
t.pickthall: And (have ye not) dwelt in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves (of old) and (hath it not) become plain to you how We dealt with them and made examples for you
t.arberry: And you dwelt in the dwelling-places of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We did with them, and how We struck similitudes for you
t.hilali-khan: And you dwelt in the dwellings of men who wronged themselves, and it was clear to you how We had dealt with them. And We put forth (many) parables for you
t.itani: And you inhabited the homes of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, and We cited for you the examples.
t.mubarakpuri: "And you dwelt in the dwellings of men who wronged themselves, and it was clear to you how We dealt with them. And We put forth (many) parables for you."
t.qarib: You have lived in the same places as those who wronged themselves, and it had become clear to you how We dealt with them and We gave you many examples
t.wahiduddin: You lived in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it was made clear to you how We had dealt with them and We gave you many examples
t.shakir: And you dwell in the abodes of those who were unjust to themselves, and it is clear to you how We dealt with them and We have made (them) examples to you
t.haleem: You lived in the same places as others who wronged themselves before, and you were clearly shown how We dealt with them––We gave you many examples
t.maariful: And you dwelt in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, and We put forth for you the examples.”
t.khattab: You passed by the ruins of those ˹destroyed peoples˺ who had wronged themselves. It was made clear to you how We dealt with them, and We gave you ˹many˺ examples
t.ghali: And you dwelt in the dwellings of the ones who did injustice to themselves, and it became evident to you how We performed with them, and We have struck similitudes for you
t.irving: You have inhabited the dwellings of those who wronged themselves; it was explained to you how We had dealt with them, and We made up parables for you
t.iwq: And you lived among the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it had become clear to you how We dealt with them. And We presented for you many examples

t_id.kemenag: dan kamu telah berdiam di tempat-tempat kediaman orang-orang yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri, dan telah nyata bagimu bagaimana Kami telah berbuat terhadap mereka dan telah Kami berikan kepadamu beberapa perumpamaan".
t_id.sabiq: Kalian di dunia tinggal di tempat bangsa-bangsa yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri dengan kekafiran dan perbuatan maksiat. Kemudian, dari peninggalan-peninggalan mereka kalian mengetahui bagaimana Kami menyiksa mereka, tetapi kalian tidak merasa gentar. Di samping itu, Kami telah menerangkan kepada kalian ciri-ciri pekerjaan yang mereka lakukan dan hal-hal yang menimpa mereka, tetapi kalian tidak mengambilnya sebagai pelajaran."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Dan kalian telah berdiam) di dunia (di tempat-tempat kediaman orang-orang yang menganiaya dirinya sendiri) dengan melakukan kekafiran, yaitu bekas tempat tinggal umat-umat terdahulu yang durhaka (dan telah nyata bagi kalian bagaimana Kami telah berbuat terhadap mereka) yaitu berupa siksaan, akan tetapi kalian masih tetap tidak mau kapok juga (dan telah Kami berikan) telah Kami jelaskan (kepada kalian beberapa perumpamaan.") di dalam Alquran, akan tetapi kalian tidak mau mengambilnya sebagai pelajaran.
t_id.iwq: dan kamu telah berdiam di tempat-tempat kediaman orang-orang yang menganiaya diri mereka sendiri, dan telah nyata bagimu bagaimana Kami telah berbuat terhadap mereka dan telah Kami berikan kepadamu beberapa perumpamaan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وسكنتم» فيها «في مساكن الذين ظلموا أنفسهم» بالكفر من الأمم السابقة «وتبين لكم كيف فعلنا بهم» من العقوبة فلم تنزجروا «وضربنا» بينا «لكم الأمثال» في القرآن فلم تعتبروا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَسَكَنتُمۡ فِي مَسَٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓاْ أَنفُسَهُمۡ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمۡ كَيۡفَ فَعَلۡنَا بِهِمۡ وَضَرَبۡنَا لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَمۡثَالَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan kalian telah berdiam) di dunia (di tempat-tempat kediaman orang-orang yang menganiaya dirinya sendiri) dengan melakukan kekafiran, yaitu bekas tempat tinggal umat-umat terdahulu yang durhaka (dan telah nyata bagi kalian bagaimana Kami telah berbuat terhadap mereka) yaitu berupa siksaan, akan tetapi kalian masih tetap tidak mau kapok juga (dan telah Kami berikan) telah Kami jelaskan (kepada kalian beberapa perumpamaan.") di dalam Alquran, akan tetapi kalian tidak mau mengambilnya sebagai pelajaran

ar: وَقَدۡ مَكَرُوا۟ مَكۡرَهُمۡ وَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَكۡرُهُمۡ وَإِن كَانَ مَكۡرُهُمۡ لِتَزُولَ مِنۡهُ ٱلۡجِبَالُ
ar_simple: وَقَدْ مَكَرُوا مَكْرَهُمْ وَعِنْدَ اللَّهِ مَكْرُهُمْ وَإِنْ كَانَ مَكْرُهُمْ لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ الْجِبَالُ

t.sahih-int: And they had planned their plan, but with Allah is [recorded] their plan, even if their plan had been [sufficient] to do away with the mountains
t.asad: And [this retribution will befall all evildoers because] they devise that false imagery of theirs - and all their false imagery is within God's knowledge. [And never can the blasphemers prevail against the truth - not] even if their false imagery were so (well devised and so powerful] that mountains could be moved thereby
t.yusuf-ali: Mighty indeed were the plots which they made, but their plots were (well) within the sight of Allah, even though they were such as to shake the hills
t.pickthall: Verily they have plotted their plot, and their plot is with Allah, though their plot were one whereby the mountains should be moved
t.arberry: They devised their devising, and their devising is known to God; though their devising were such as to remove mountains
t.hilali-khan: Indeed, they planned their plot, and their plot was with Allah, though their plot was a great (one, still) it would never be able to remove the mountains (real mountains or the Islamic law) from their places (as it is of no importance) [Tafsir Ibn Kathir, Vol. 2, Page 597]. [It is said by some interpreters regarding this Verse that the Quraish pagans plotted against Prophet Muhammad SAW to kill him but they failed and were unable to carry out their plot which they plotted]
t.itani: They planned their plans, but their plans are known to God, even if their plans can eliminate mountains.
t.mubarakpuri: Indeed, they planned their plot, and their plot was with Allah, though their plot was not such as to remove the mountains from their places.
t.qarib: They planned their schemes, but God was aware of their scheming, even when their schemes were such as to make mountains vanish
t.wahiduddin: They hatched their plots; but these plots were all within God's knowledge. Though their plots were such as to shake the mountains, God will bring their plots to nothing
t.shakir: And they have indeed planned their plan, but their plan is with Allah, though their plan was such that the mountains should pass away thereby
t.haleem: They made their plots, but, even if their plots had been able to move mountains, God had the answer
t.maariful: And they worked out their plot and whatever they plot is before Allah, even though their plot is such as would move the mountains
t.khattab: They devised every plot, which was fully known to Allah, but their plotting was not enough to ˹even˺ overpower mountains ˹let alone Allah˺
t.ghali: And they readily schemed their scheming, and their scheming is in the Providence of Allah, and decidedly their scheming was such that thereby mountains should be removed
t.irving: They hatched their plot while God held up their plot, even though their plan had been to transport the mountains by means of it
t.iwq: And they had planned their plan, but with Allah is recorded their plan, even if their plan had been sufficient to do away with the mountains

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membuat makar yang besar padahal di sisi Allah-lah (balasan) makar mereka itu. Dan sesungguhnya makar mereka itu (amat besar) sehingga gunung-gunung dapat lenyap karenanya.
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang musyrik itu telah berbuat makar untuk menggagalkan dakwah. Allah mengetahui makar mereka. Makar itu tidak akan melenyapkan syariat yang kokoh, sekokoh gunung.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membuat makar) terhadap Nabi saw. (makar yang besar) yaitu mereka bermaksud untuk membunuh Nabi saw. atau membelenggunya, atau mengusirnya (padahal di sisi Allahlah makar mereka) yakni pengetahuan makar tersebut, atau pembalasan makar itu. (Dan tidak akan) tidak bisa (makar mereka itu) betapa pun besarnya (dapat melenyapkan gunung-gunung) pengertiannya ialah makar tersebut dibiarkan dan tidak memberikan mudarat melainkan hanya terhadap diri mereka sendiri. Yang dimaksud dengan pengertian gunung-gunung di sini, menurut suatu pendapat adalah hakiki, yakni gunung yang sesungguhnya, dan menurut pendapat yang lain dimaksud adalah syariat-syariat Islam yang digambarkan seperti gunung-gunung dalam hal ketetapan dan keteguhannya. Menurut suatu qiraat yang lain litazuula ini dibaca latazuulu, yakni dengan harakat fatah pada huruf lamnya kemudian akhir fi'ilnya dibaca rafa', maka berdasarkan qiraat ini berarti huruf in di sini adalah bentuk takhfif atau keringanan daripada huruf inna yang ditasydidkan huruf nunnya, makna yang dimaksud adalah menggambarkan tentang besarnya makar orang-orang kafir itu terhadap diri Nabi saw. Akan tetapi menurut pendapat yang lain dikatakan, yang dimaksud dengan lafal al-makru ialah kekafiran mereka. Makna yang terakhir ini sesuai pula dengan apa yang disebutkan di dalam firman Allah swt. yang. lainnya, yaitu, "Hampir-hampir langit pecah karena ucapan itu (mendakwa Tuhan mempunyai anak), bumi belah, dan gunung-gunung runtuh." (Q.S. Maryam 90) Sedangkan pengertian yang pertama sesuai dengan bacaan yang tertera.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membuat makar yang besar padahal di sisi Allah-lah (balasan) makar mereka itu. Dan sesungguhnya makar mereka itu (amat besar) sehingga gunung-gunung dapat lenyap karenanya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (وقد مكروا) بالنبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (مكرهم) حيث أرادوا قتله أو تقييده أو إخراجه (وعند الله مكرهم) أي علمه أو جزاؤه (وإن) ما (كان مكرهم) وإن عظم (لتزول منه الجبال) المعنى لا يعبأ به ولا يضر إلا أنفسهم والمراد بالجبال هنا قيل حقيقتها وقيل شرائع الإسلام المشبهة بها في القرار والثبات وفي قراءة بفتح لام لتزول ورفع الفعل فإن مخففة والمراد تعظيم مكرهم وقيل المراد بالمكر كفرهم ويناسبه على الثانية "" تكاد السماوات يتفطرن منه وتنشق الأرض وتخر الجبال هدا "" وعلى الأول ما قرئ وما كان
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَدۡ مَكَرُواْ مَكۡرَهُمۡ وَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَكۡرُهُمۡ وَإِن كَانَ مَكۡرُهُمۡ لِتَزُولَ مِنۡهُ ٱلۡجِبَالُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membuat makar) terhadap Nabi saw. (makar yang besar) yaitu mereka bermaksud untuk membunuh Nabi saw. atau membelenggunya, atau mengusirnya (padahal di sisi Allahlah makar mereka) yakni pengetahuan makar tersebut, atau pembalasan makar itu. (Dan tidak akan) tidak bisa (makar mereka itu) betapa pun besarnya (dapat melenyapkan gunung-gunung) pengertiannya ialah makar tersebut dibiarkan dan tidak memberikan mudarat melainkan hanya terhadap diri mereka sendiri. Yang dimaksud dengan pengertian gunung-gunung di sini, menurut suatu pendapat adalah hakiki, yakni gunung yang sesungguhnya, dan menurut pendapat yang lain dimaksud adalah syariat-syariat Islam yang digambarkan seperti gunung-gunung dalam hal ketetapan dan keteguhannya. Menurut suatu qiraat yang lain litazuula ini dibaca latazuulu, yakni dengan harakat fatah pada huruf lamnya kemudian akhir fi'ilnya dibaca rafa', maka berdasarkan qiraat ini berarti huruf in di sini adalah bentuk takhfif atau keringanan daripada huruf inna yang ditasydidkan huruf nunnya, makna yang dimaksud adalah menggambarkan tentang besarnya makar orang-orang kafir itu terhadap diri Nabi saw. Akan tetapi menurut pendapat yang lain dikatakan, yang dimaksud dengan lafal al-makru ialah kekafiran mereka. Makna yang terakhir ini sesuai pula dengan apa yang disebutkan di dalam firman Allah swt. yang. lainnya, yaitu, "Hampir-hampir langit pecah karena ucapan itu (mendakwa Tuhan mempunyai anak), bumi belah, dan gunung-gunung runtuh." (Q.S. Maryam 90) Sedangkan pengertian yang pertama sesuai dengan bacaan yang tertera

ar: فَلَا تَحۡسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخۡلِفَ وَعۡدِهِۦ رُسُلَهُۥۤۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِیزࣱ ذُو ٱنتِقَامࣲ
ar_simple: فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِ رُسُلَهُ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انْتِقَامٍ

t.sahih-int: So never think that Allah will fail in His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Owner of Retribution
t.asad: HENCE, do not think that God will fail to fulfil the promise which He gave to His apostles: verily, God is almighty, an avenger of evil
t.yusuf-ali: Never think that Allah would fail his messengers in His promise: for Allah is Exalted in power, - the Lord of Retribution
t.pickthall: So think not that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His messengers. Lo! Allah is Mighty, Able to Requite (the wrong)
t.arberry: So do not deem that God will fail in His promise to His Messengers; surely God is All-mighty, Vengeful
t.hilali-khan: So think not that Allah will fail to keep His Promise to His Messengers. Certainly, Allah is All-Mighty, - All-Able of Retribution
t.itani: Do not ever think that God will break His promise to His messengers. God is Strong, Able to Avenge.
t.mubarakpuri: So think not that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His Messengers. Certainly, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Able of Retribution.
t.qarib: Do not ever think that God will fail to fulfill His promise to His Messengers. God is Almighty, Exactor of Revenge
t.wahiduddin: Never think that God will fail in His promise to His messengers. God is mighty and capable of retribution
t.shakir: Therefore do not think Allah (to be one) failing in His promise to His apostles; surely Allah is Mighty, the Lord of Retribution
t.haleem: So do not think [Prophet] that God will break His promise to His messengers: He is mighty, and capable of retribution
t.maariful: So, never think that Allah will do against His promise to His messengers. Surely, Allah is Mighty, the Lord of Retribution
t.khattab: So do not think ˹O Prophet˺ that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His messengers. Allah is indeed Almighty, capable of punishment
t.ghali: So do definitely not reckon that Allah will fail in His promise to His Messengers; surely Allah is Ever-Mighty, Owner of vengeance
t.irving: Do not reckon God will break His promise to His messengers; God is Powerful, the Wielder of Retribution
t.iwq: Thus never think that Allah will fail in His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Owner of Retribution

t_id.kemenag: Karena itu janganlah sekali-kali kamu mengira Allah akan menyalahi janji-Nya kepada rasul-raaul-Nya; sesungguhnya Allah Maha Perkasa, lagi mempunyai pembalasan.
t_id.sabiq: Janganlah engkau mengira, wahai Rasulullah, bahwa Allah Swt. tidak menepati janji kemenangan yang pernah diberikan kepada rasul-rasul-Nya. Dia Mahaperkasa, tidak ada yang mampu menghalangi kehendak- Nya, dan sangat keras pembalasan-Nya terhadap orang yang kafir dan mendurhakai rasul-rasul-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Karena itu janganlah sekali-kali kalian mengira Allah akan menyalahi janji-Nya kepada rasul-rasul-Nya) yaitu akan memberikan pertolongan kepadanya. (Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Perkasa) Maha Menang, tiada sesuatu pun yang dapat menghalang-halangi-Nya (lagi mempunyai pembalasan) terhadap orang-orang yang mendurhakai-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Karena itu janganlah sekali-kali kamu mengira Allah akan menyalahi janji-Nya kepada rasul-raaul-Nya; sesungguhnya Allah Maha Perkasa, lagi mempunyai pembalasan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلا تحسبنَّ الله مخلف وعده رسله» بالنصر «إن الله عزيز» غالب لا يعجزه شيء «ذو انتقام» ممن عصاه
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَا تَحۡسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخۡلِفَ وَعۡدِهِۦ رُسُلَهُۥٓۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٞ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Karena itu janganlah sekali-kali kalian mengira Allah akan menyalahi janji-Nya kepada rasul-rasul-Nya) yaitu akan memberikan pertolongan kepadanya. (Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Perkasa) Maha Menang, tiada sesuatu pun yang dapat menghalang-halangi-Nya (lagi mempunyai pembalasan) terhadap orang-orang yang mendurhakai-Nya

ar: یَوۡمَ تُبَدَّلُ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ غَیۡرَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰتُۖ وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلۡوَ ٰحِدِ ٱلۡقَهَّارِ
ar_simple: يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ الْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ الْأَرْضِ وَالسَّمَاوَاتُ ۖ وَبَرَزُوا لِلَّهِ الْوَاحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ

t.sahih-int: [It will be] on the Day the earth will be replaced by another earth, and the heavens [as well], and all creatures will come out before Allah, the One, the Prevailing
t.asad: [His promise will be fulfilled] on the Day when the earth shall be changed into another earth, as shall be the heavens and when [all men] shall appear before God, the One who holds absolute sway over all that exists
t.yusuf-ali: One day the earth will be changed to a different earth, and so will be the heavens, and (men) will be marshalled forth, before Allah, the One, the Irresistible
t.pickthall: On the day when the earth will be changed to other than the earth, and the heavens (also will be changed) and they will come forth unto Allah, the One, the Almighty
t.arberry: Upon the day the earth shall be changed to other than the earth, and the heavens and they sally forth unto God, the One, the Omnipotent
t.hilali-khan: On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will be the heavens, and they (all creatures) will appear before Allah, the One, the Irresistible
t.itani: On the Day when the earth is changed into another earth, and the heavens, and they will emerge before God, the One, the Irresistible.
t.mubarakpuri: On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will be the heavens, and they (all creatures) will appear before Allah, the One, the Irresistible.
t.qarib: [It will be fulfilled] on the Day when the earth is changed to other than the earth, and the heavens too, and they all appear before God, the One, the All-Conquering
t.wahiduddin: On the Day when the earth shall be changed into another earth, as shall be the heavens, they will all appear before God, the One, the Most Supreme
t.shakir: On the day when the earth shall be changed into a different earth, and the heavens (as well), and they shall come forth before Allah, the One, the Supreme
t.haleem: One Day- when the earth is turned into another earth, the heavens into another heaven, and people all appear before God, the One, the Overpowering
t.maariful: the day on which this earth will be turned into some other earth, and the skies as well, and they all shall appear before Allah, the One, the All-Dominant
t.khattab: ˹Watch for˺ the Day ˹when˺ the earth will be changed into a different earth and the heavens as well, and all will appear before Allah—the One, the Supreme
t.ghali: Upon the Day the earth will be exchanged to other than the earth and the heavens (will be exchanged); and they will go forth to Allah, The One, The Superb Vanquisher
t.irving: Some day the earth will be changed into a different earth, and [so will] Heaven; they will emerge for God Alone, the Irresistible
t.iwq: It will be on the Day the earth will be replaced by another earth, and the heavens as well, and all creatures will come out before Allah, the One, the Prevailing

t_id.kemenag: (Yaitu) pada hari (ketika) bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan (demikian pula) langit, dan meraka semuanya (di padang Mahsyar) berkumpul menghadap ke hadirat Allah yang Maha Esa lagi Maha Perkasa.
t_id.sabiq: Dari itu, Dia akan membalas mereka pada hari kiamat, saat bumi dan langit yang ada sekarang ini digantikan dengan bumi dan langit yang lain, dan semua makhluk keluar dari dalam kubur untuk pengadilan Allah yang tidak bersekutu dan tidak terkalahkan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Ingatlah (Pada hari ketika bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan demikian pula langit) yaitu hari kiamat, kemudian manusia digiring untuk dikumpulkan di suatu tanah yang putih bersih sebagaimana yang telah disebutkan di dalam hadis kitab Sahih Bukhari dan Muslim. Sehubungan dengan hal ini Imam Muslim meriwayatkan sebuah hadis bahwasanya Nabi saw. ditanya mengenai manusia pada saat itu. Lalu Nabi saw. menjawab, "Berada di shirath." (dan mereka semuanya tampak bermunculan) artinya mereka keluar dari kuburan mereka masing-masing (untuk menghadap kepada Allah Yang Maha Esa lagi Maha Perkasa.)
t_id.iwq: (Yaitu) pada hari (ketika) bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan (demikian pula) langit, dan meraka semuanya (di padang Mahsyar) berkumpul menghadap ke hadirat Allah yang Maha Esa lagi Maha Perkasa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: اذكر (يوم تبدل الأرض غير الأرض والسماوات) هو يوم القيامة فيحشر الناس على أرض بيضاء نقية كما في حديث الصحيحين وروى مسلم حديث: سئل النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم أين الناس يومئذ قال: "" على الصراط "" (وبرزوا) خرجوا من القبور (لله الواحد القهار)
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَوۡمَ تُبَدَّلُ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ غَيۡرَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتُۖ وَبَرَزُواْ لِلَّهِ ٱلۡوَٰحِدِ ٱلۡقَهَّارِ (وَبَرَزُوا: خَرَجُوا ظَاهِرِينَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Ingatlah (Pada hari ketika bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan demikian pula langit) yaitu hari kiamat, kemudian manusia digiring untuk dikumpulkan di suatu tanah yang putih bersih sebagaimana yang telah disebutkan di dalam hadis kitab Sahih Bukhari dan Muslim. Sehubungan dengan hal ini Imam Muslim meriwayatkan sebuah hadis bahwasanya Nabi saw. ditanya mengenai manusia pada saat itu. Lalu Nabi saw. menjawab, "Berada di shirath." (dan mereka semuanya tampak bermunculan) artinya mereka keluar dari kuburan mereka masing-masing (untuk menghadap kepada Allah Yang Maha Esa lagi Maha Perkasa)

ar: وَتَرَى ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِینَ یَوۡمَئِذࣲ مُّقَرَّنِینَ فِی ٱلۡأَصۡفَادِ
ar_simple: وَتَرَى الْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِي الْأَصْفَادِ

t.sahih-int: And you will see the criminals that Day bound together in shackles
t.asad: For on that Day thou wilt see all who were lost in sin linked together in fetters
t.yusuf-ali: And thou wilt see the sinners that day bound together in fetters
t.pickthall: Thou wilt see the guilty on that day linked together in chains
t.arberry: And thou shalt see the sinners that day coupled in fetters
t.hilali-khan: And you will see the Mujrimun (criminals, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism, polytheists, disobedient to Allah, etc.) that Day bound together in fetters; [Muqarranun in fetters; mean:- with their hands and feet tied to their necks with chains]
t.itani: On that Day, you will see the sinners bound together in chains.
t.mubarakpuri: And you will see the criminals that Day, Muqarranun (bound together) in fetters.
t.qarib: For on that Day, you will see all those who force others to reject God's messages linked together in chains
t.wahiduddin: On that Day you shall see the guilty bound in chains
t.shakir: And you will see the guilty on that day linked together in chains
t.haleem: you [Prophet] will see the guilty on that Day, bound together in fetters
t.maariful: On that day you will see the culprits tied up together in chains
t.khattab: On that Day you will see the wicked bound together in chains
t.ghali: And you will see the criminals upon that Day interlinked in the fetters
t.irving: You will see criminals linked together by means of fetters on that day
t.iwq: And you will see the criminals that Day bound together in shackles

t_id.kemenag: Dan kamu akan melihat orang-orang yang berdosa pada hari itu diikat bersama-sama dengan belenggu.
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari kiamat itu, engkau akan melihat orang-orang kafir dibelenggu dengan tali bersama setan- setan mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan kamu akan melihat) hai Muhammad (orang-orang yang berdosa) yakni orang-orang kafir (pada hari itu diikat) dalam keadaan terikat beserta setan-setan mereka (dengan belenggu) tali-tali atau rantai-rantai.
t_id.iwq: Dan kamu akan melihat orang-orang yang berdosa pada hari itu diikat bersama-sama dengan belenggu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وترى» يا محمد تبصر «المجرمين» الكافرين «يومئذ مقرنين» مشدودين مع شياطينهم «في الأصفاد» القيود أو الأغلال
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَتَرَى ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ يَوۡمَئِذٖ مُّقَرَّنِينَ فِي ٱلۡأَصۡفَادِ (مُّقَرَّنِينَ فِي الأَصْفَادِ: مُقَيَّدِينَ بِالقُيُودِ، قَدْ قُرِنَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُمْ بِالسَّلَاسِلِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan kamu akan melihat) hai Muhammad (orang-orang yang berdosa) yakni orang-orang kafir (pada hari itu diikat) dalam keadaan terikat beserta setan-setan mereka (dengan belenggu) tali-tali atau rantai-rantai

ar: سَرَابِیلُهُم مِّن قَطِرَانࣲ وَتَغۡشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ
ar_simple: سَرَابِيلُهُمْ مِنْ قَطِرَانٍ وَتَغْشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ

t.sahih-int: Their garments of liquid pitch and their faces covered by the Fire
t.asad: clothed in garments of black pitch, with fire veiling their faces
t.yusuf-ali: Their garments of liquid pitch, and their faces covered with Fire
t.pickthall: Their raiment of pitch, and the Fire covering their faces
t.arberry: of pitch their shirts, their faces enveloped by the Fire
t.hilali-khan: Their garments will be of pitch, and fire will cover their faces
t.itani: Their garments made of tar, and the Fire covering their faces.
t.mubarakpuri: Their garments will be of Qatiran (tar), and fire will cover their faces.
t.qarib: clothed in garments of tar, with fire veiling their faces
t.wahiduddin: their garments shall be of pitch and the fire shall envelop their faces
t.shakir: Their shirts made of pitch and the fire covering their faces
t.haleem: in garments of pitch, faces covered in fire
t.maariful: their shirts will be of rosin, and their faces will be covered with Fire
t.khattab: with garments of tar, and their faces covered with flames
t.ghali: Their apparels of pitch, and their faces enveloped by the Fire
t.irving: their garments will be [smeared] with tar while fire will cover their faces
t.iwq: Their garments of liquid pitch and their faces covered by the Fire

t_id.kemenag: Pakaian mereka adalah dari pelangkin (ter) dan muka mereka ditutup oleh api neraka,
t_id.sabiq: Sekujur tubuh mereka dibalur dengan cairan sejenis tir, hingga seolah-olah seperti pakaian yang melekat di badan mereka, sementara wajah mereka tertutupi api.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pakaian mereka) baju-baju mereka (adalah dari aspal) yang sangat mudah menyala (dan tertutuplah) ditutuplah (muka mereka oleh api neraka).
t_id.iwq: Pakaian mereka adalah dari pelangkin (ter) dan muka mereka ditutup oleh api neraka,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «سرابيلهم» قمصهم «من قطران» لأنه أبلغ لاشتعال النار «وتغشى» تعلو «وجوههم النار»
tafsir.siraj-ar: سَرَابِيلُهُم مِّن قَطِرَانٖ وَتَغۡشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ (سَرَابِيلُهُمْ: ثِيَابُهُمْ, قَطِرَانٍ: مَادَّةٍ شَدِيدَةِ الِاشْتِعَالِ، تُشْبِهُ الزِّفْتَ، سَوْدَاءِ اللَّوْنِ مُنْتِنَةِ الرِّيحِ, وَتَغْشَى: تَعْلُو، وَتَلْفَحُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pakaian mereka) baju-baju mereka (adalah dari aspal) yang sangat mudah menyala (dan tertutuplah) ditutuplah (muka mereka oleh api neraka)

ar: لِیَجۡزِیَ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفۡسࣲ مَّا كَسَبَتۡۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِیعُ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
ar_simple: لِيَجْزِيَ اللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَا كَسَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ

t.sahih-int: So that Allah will recompense every soul for what it earned. Indeed, Allah is swift in account
t.asad: [And all shall be judged on that Day,] so that God may requite every human being for all that he has earned [in life]: verily, God is swift in reckoning
t.yusuf-ali: That Allah may requite each soul according to its deserts; and verily Allah is swift in calling to account
t.pickthall: That Allah may repay each soul what it hath earned. Lo! Allah is swift at reckoning
t.arberry: that God may recompense every soul for its earnings; surely God is swift at the reckoning
t.hilali-khan: That Allah may requite each person according to what he has earned. Truly, Allah is Swift at reckoning
t.itani: That God may repay each soul according to what it has earned. God is Quick in reckoning.
t.mubarakpuri: That Allah may requite each person according to what he has earned. Truly, Allah is swift at reckoning.
t.qarib: [All will be judged on that Day], so that God may reward every soul for what it earned: God is swift in His reckoning
t.wahiduddin: God will requite each soul according to its deeds. Swift is God's reckoning
t.shakir: That Allah may requite each soul (according to) what it has earned; surely Allah is swift in reckoning
t.haleem: [All will be judged] so that God may reward each soul as it deserves: God is swift in His reckoning
t.maariful: so that Allah repays everyone in full whatever he earned. Indeed, Allah is swift at reckoning
t.khattab: As such, Allah will reward every soul for what it has committed. Surely Allah is swift in reckoning
t.ghali: That Allah may recompense every self for what it has earned; surely Allah is Swift at the reckoning
t.irving: so God may reward each soul according to whatever it has earned. God is Prompt at reckoning
t.iwq: Thus that Allah will recompense every soul for what it earned. Indeed, Allah is swift in account

t_id.kemenag: agar Allah memberi pembalasan kepada tiap-tiap orang terhadap apa yang ia usahakan. Sesungguhnya Allah Maha cepat hisab-Nya.
t_id.sabiq: Allah berbuat begitu untuk membalas tiap orang sesuai dengan perbuatan yang pernah ia lakukan di dunia. Perhitungan Allah pada hari kiamat sangat cepat, dan tidak ada sesuatu pun yang dapat mengganggu-Nya dalam melakukan hal itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Agar pembalasan diberikan) lafal ini bertaalluq kepada lafal barazuu (oleh Allah kepada tiap-tiap orang terhadap apa yang ia usahakan) baik berupa kebaikan atau pun keburukan. (Sesungguhnya Allah Maha cepat hisab-Nya) Dia menghisab semua makhluk selama setengah hari menurut ukuran hari dunia, demikianlah menurut penjelasan hadis.
t_id.iwq: agar Allah memberi pembalasan kepada tiap-tiap orang terhadap apa yang ia usahakan. Sesungguhnya Allah Maha cepat hisab-Nya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ليجزي» متعلق ببرزوا «الله كل نفس ما كسبت» من خير وشر «إن الله سريع الحساب» يحاسب جميع الخلق في قدر نصف نهار من أيام الدنيا لحديث بذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: لِيَجۡزِيَ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفۡسٖ مَّا كَسَبَتۡۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Agar pembalasan diberikan) lafal ini bertaalluq kepada lafal barazuu (oleh Allah kepada tiap-tiap orang terhadap apa yang ia usahakan) baik berupa kebaikan atau pun keburukan. (Sesungguhnya Allah Maha cepat hisab-Nya) Dia menghisab semua makhluk selama setengah hari menurut ukuran hari dunia, demikianlah menurut penjelasan hadis

ar: هَـٰذَا بَلَـٰغࣱ لِّلنَّاسِ وَلِیُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَلِیَعۡلَمُوۤا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهࣱ وَ ٰحِدࣱ وَلِیَذَّكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلۡأَلۡبَـٰبِ
